prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response:
The fog rolled in suddenly, swallowing the lighthouse and the storm-tossed sea whole. Thomas had worked the beacon for thirty years, yet this particular autumn morning felt different—the thick mist carried an unusual stillness.

As he made his rounds, lamp in hand, the beam caught something unnatural amid the swirling gray. A figure, kneeling upon the rocks below. Thomas descended cautiously, his boots finding precarious footholds on the cliffside. As he drew nearer, his heart fairly stopped—it was a woman, her form shifting inexplicably between solid and translucent with each passing beam of light from the tower above.

She didn't turn but continued her strange ritual, hands pressing wet sand into shapes that reminded Thomas of coastal maps from centuries past. When he reached the bottom, she vanished, leaving only precise indentations and a small, perfect seashell that pulsed with a soft inner light—something that had never before existed on this shoreline.

He pocketed the shell, then one of the sand creations disintegrated into the sea. Above, the fog lifted as if by command, revealing a horizon that somehow felt expanded—a new depth to the ocean, deeper than holding the legacy of his father and grandfather would ever permit him to comprehend.
